@slideforge
Carousel
CORE DEFINITION

Cialdini's Seven Principles

/si-əl-DI-niz ˈsɛvən ˈprɪnsəpəlz/

Robert Cialdini's framework of seven universal persuasion principles: Reciprocity, Commitment, Social Proof, Authority, Liking, Scarcity, and Unity.

Every effective campaign implicitly or explicitly uses these principles. Making them explicit turns persuasion from art into engineering.

SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Reciprocity is why free content converts better than paid advertising.

Principle #1: Reciprocity — Give value first. The audience feels obligated to reciprocate — with attention, engagement, or action.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Myth vs Fact

Scarcity only works for physical products
MYTH
FACT
Information scarcity ('this insight won't be shared again') drives 2.8x more engagement than abundance framing
Scarcity is about perceived value, not physical limitation. Limited-time insights, exclusive data, and 'first to know' framing all trigger the same response.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Social proof is why testimonial_avatar is one of the most-used slide types.

Principle #2: Social Proof — People follow people. Testimonials, user counts, and 'trusted by' logos reduce decision friction.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Myth vs Fact

Authority requires credentials
MYTH
FACT
Perceived authority comes from confidence and specificity, not titles. 'We analyzed 10,000 slides' beats 'our expert says.'
Authority is established through demonstrated knowledge, not claimed credentials. Data beats titles.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Commitment explains why carousel completion rate predicts conversion better than click-through rate.

Principle #3: Commitment — Small yeses lead to big yeses. Each micro-commitment (save, share, read) escalates investment.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Myth vs Fact

Liking is about being likeable
MYTH
FACT
Liking is about similarity and shared identity. 'People like me use this' is more powerful than 'this person is charismatic.'
The liking principle works through identification, not charm. That's why founder stories outperform celebrity endorsements for niche products.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Unity is Cialdini's most overlooked principle — and the most powerful for building movements.

Principle #4: Unity — Shared identity. 'We' language. 'Our community.' Unity turns customers into members and members into advocates.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Myth vs Fact

Unity is just branding
MYTH
FACT
Unity is identity-level belonging. Branding is visual. Unity is tribal. 'I am a Nexus user' vs 'I use Nexus.'
Unity explains why open-source communities outperform proprietary user bases in advocacy and retention.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Liking explains why founder-led carousels outperform corporate-polished ones.

Principle #5: Liking — People say yes to those they like. Similarity, compliments, and cooperation build the liking bridge.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Myth vs Fact

Scarcity and urgency are the same thing
MYTH
FACT
Scarcity is about limited supply; urgency is about limited time. Combined, they create the most powerful persuasion force in marketing.
The best CTAs use both: 'Only 3 spots left' (scarcity) + 'Offer ends tonight' (urgency). Together they're exponentially more effective.
SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Apply the Principles

01
Audit
Map which principles your current content uses (and which it ignores)
02
Integrate
Assign one principle per slide. Let the preset structure do the sequencing.
03
Measure
Track which principles drive the most engagement for your audience
04
Iterate
Double down on what works. Drop what doesn't.

Automated 3-step compilation pipeline converting raw JSON specifications into production-ready carousel assets.

SLIDEFORGE PRINCIPLE EDUCATION
slideforge.dev #slides #campaign

Implementation Checklist

1
Identify your primary principle
2
Map principles to slide types
3
Generate with a preset
4
A/B test principle emphasis
